PublicRemarks: Welcome to 18 Vernon Rd in Havertown! This renovated red brick row home offers a wonderful opportunity to own in Havertown! Walking up to the home you'll step up to an enclosed front porch with ceiling fan & coat closet before entering the first floor through a beautiful french door. The original wood floors & high ceilings quickly grab your attention and make the spaces feel generous and welcoming! The first floor offers a nice sized living room, formal dining room, carefully located 1st floor 1/2 bath, and a renovated galley style kitchen with beautiful wood cabinetry, stainless steel appliances (gas stove, dishwasher, refrigerator & microwave), granite tops, subway tile backsplash, and under-mount sink. A door off the back of the kitchen leads to the back patio and fenced rear yard with white 6' vinyl fencing & back gate. Heading back inside, the 2nd floor presents 3 bedrooms, all with ceiling fans, and a renovated full hall bath with full tub, tile floor and surround. Stairs off the dining room lead down to a full unfinished basement that includes front loading washer and dryer. The space offers great storage and an opportunity to expand the living space with good ceiling height and plenty of square footage to work with. Other great features of the home include central air, gas heat & hot water, re-wired electrical system, and insulated windows.  Located a 1/2 block from entrance of the newly opened Pennsy Trail extension, you can walk or ride a bike to the middle of Havertown in just minutes! This home sits just minutes from restaurants and shopping along W. Chester Pike (Rt 3), as well as, Route 1, including the Quarry Center, Manoa Shopping Center, Brookline, and more.  Don't miss this opportunity!

PublicRemarks: There are homes that check boxes and then there are homes that create a feeling. This is the latter. Set quietly atop 1.3 exquisitely landscaped acres in the coveted Harrison Estate enclave, this European inspired residence is a study in restraint, proportion, and presence, where every detail has been considered and nothing is accidental. The approach is discreet yet impressive, opening to a circular drive and a home that reveals itself slowly, the way all truly special properties do. Inside, nearly 7,000 square feet of interiors unfold with quiet drama. Sunlight pours through expansive windows, casting a warm southern glow across rich wood, marble, and tailored finishes. A breathtaking two story foyer introduces a sculptural, hand forged iron staircase by M. Cohen & Sons, less a feature, more a piece of art, setting an immediate tone of craftsmanship and permanence. The living spaces are both elevated and livable. A vaulted family room, anchored by a custom stone fireplace and artisan built millwork by Don Green, invites gathering without formality. The dining room, warmed by a Chesney fireplace, was designed for lingering evenings and effortless entertaining. And at the heart of it all, a true chef’s kitchen, equipped with Viking and Sub Zero appliances, functions as both a workspace and a social hub, seamlessly connecting to a sunlit breakfast room and beyond. Then, the moment everything opens up. French doors lead to a sweeping flagstone terrace, perfectly oriented to the south, where the landscape becomes part of the living experience. The gardens, lush, natural, and thoughtfully curated to be pesticide free, create a setting that feels both grounded and transportive. The pool, with its twin Badu swim jets, offers the rare luxury of both relaxation and performance, all set against uninterrupted hilltop views that shift beautifully with the seasons. Upstairs, the primary suite is not just private, it is restorative. A sitting room, its own balcony, dual walk in closets, and a Carrara marble bath come together to create a retreat that feels closer to a boutique hotel than a residence. Additional bedrooms are generously proportioned and quietly refined, while a finished walk up attic offers flexibility for guests, studio space, or retreat. The lower level continues the narrative of thoughtful living, a fully realized extension of the home with a media lounge, game room, kitchenette, fitness space, and full bath, designed not as an afterthought, but as an integral part of the lifestyle. Behind the scenes, every modern convenience is in place, multi zone climate control, whole home connectivity, security, generator, ensuring comfort and confidence without ever intruding on the aesthetic. And yet, for all its privacy and serenity, the home remains remarkably connected, just minutes to the Main Line’s most prestigious schools, dining destinations, shopping, Center City Philadelphia, and Philadelphia International Airport. This is a home for someone who understands that true luxury is not loud. It is felt. It is lived. And once experienced, it is not easily forgotten. Photos and Media Coming Soon!

PublicRemarks: Welcome to 258 New Darlington Road situated on a hilltop off a private lane overlooking the Darlington Valley, this is a sunny 5-bedroom, 4 full and 2 half bath single-family home located in the desirable Rose Tree Media School District in Media. As you approach the home, a well-manicured yard with specimen trees leads to the front entry. Inside, the foyer features hardwood floors, crown molding, and chair rail with wainscoting, along with French doors that open to the back deck. To the right, the living room offers hardwood floors, crown molding, and a wood-burning fireplace with a marble surround. The adjacent office includes carpet flooring, a ceiling fan, and dual entry access with French doors. A convenient powder room is also located on this side of the home. To the left of the foyer, the dining room features hardwood floors, a tray ceiling, crown molding, and chair rail with wainscoting. A butler’s pantry with wet bar, cabinets and convenient closet space provides a seamless transition into the kitchen, which includes hardwood floors, granite countertops, a center island, double ovens, and a pantry. The spacious breakfast room offers abundant light, vaulted ceiling, chair rail, and French doors leading to the back deck. The backyard is fully fenced in and designed for both entertaining and relaxation, featuring a two-level deck with a custom-made pergola and an additional lower-level patio surrounded by privacy and mature professional landscaping. The family room includes a vaulted ceiling, gas fireplace with a floor-to-ceiling stone surround, and carpet flooring. A mudroom with hardwood floors, an additional staircase, and a second powder room complete the main level. Upstairs, the primary suite features carpet flooring, a tray ceiling, ceiling fan, sitting room, and a California-style walk-in closet. The en-suite bathroom includes tile floors, a step-in shower, jetted tub, and separate his and hers vanity sinks. Four additional bedrooms on this level offer comfortable space for family or guests. A full hall bathroom with tile floors, a tub and shower combination, and a double sink vanity serves this area, along with a conveniently located laundry room. The fifth bedroom includes a walk-in closet and a private en-suite bathroom with tile floors and a tub and shower combination, making it ideal for guests or extended family. The finished walk-out lower level adds significant living space with a pool table in the rec room, a wet bar complete with a refrigerator, sink, microwave and wine fridge. This home offers a sophisticated, move-in-ready lifestyle with a serene setting in an unbeatable Radnor location.

The ¼-mile drive, which is next to the Ardrossan Estate, leads into a walled courtyard with a circular drive. One enters through a limestone porch with Doric columns into the transverse, covered, ceilinged hallway with French doors leading to the terrace, reflecting pool, and two loggias. Full of light, the main floor flows into the grand living room, the library, the morning room, and the dining room. All rooms have French doors and beamed or decoratively plastered ceilings. With fireplaces in each room and rich architectural detail, this is a house made for entertaining. The kitchen is functional yet true to the home’s character, featuring custom wood cabinetry, hand-painted tile, a central island, and a high-quality range. The wood-paneled large butler’s pantry supports both everyday living and entertaining. The bright studio/office offers a separate entrance and access to the terrace. Upstairs, the home is anchored by a dramatic gallery-style hallway with soaring barrel-vaulted ceilings and abundant natural light. At each end of the upper level, two expansive primary bedroom suites create private retreats, each with its own fireplace, sitting or lounge area, bathrooms, dressing areas, and views over the gardens. There are two other large bedrooms with interior bathrooms in the main hall. Two additional bedrooms have bathrooms across the hall. There is also a second-floor laundry. Additional features include nine fireplaces in total, one gas and eight woodburning, a wine cellar, regulation-size hardball squash court, playroom, workshop, and mushroom cellar. The property has stunning mature trees, raised garden beds, garden paths, and stone walls. The residence is in an excellent state of preservation. With the exception of certain service and utility areas, the home retains its original architectural integrity. Camp Woods is an exceptional home on a well-landscaped private property in the highly sought-after Radnor School District.

PublicRemarks: This is the house everyone has been waiting for-- both "downsizers" looking for first-floor living, and those seeking wonderful amenities like top-rated schools and an engaging community.  Here it is: a Classic Cape Cod stone 3-4 bedroom home with a highly-desirable first-floor primary bedroom suite (bedroom, bath, walk-in closets and library/office). Low taxes and minimal maintenance set the stage for a comfortable existence, and a relaxing, custom-designed cedar porch with "invisible" screens" provides three-season enjoyment of the serene 1.1 acre lot. Nestled up against the house is the spacious and beautiful Pennsylvania bluestone patio area for al fresco entertaining or nature gazing. You are just minutes from a great meal at one of the excellent Wayne restaurants or hop the nearby train to a downtown evening. Even the airport is closer from this fabulous location than most area homes. Live entirely on the first floor with extra rooms to create whatever your lifestyle dictates (the den could easily be a sitting room, second office, fourth bedroom, gym or nursery) and still have ample rooms upstairs for children/guests. Almost 40 years of pride of ownership is displayed in this home. Three zone heating, top-of-the-line Trane high efficiency heat pump, high-end built-in dehumidifying system, custom master cabinetry, California closets, exterior siding and trim requiring no painting, and gorgeous landscaping with perennials galore all await you. Enjoy your morning coffee in the sunny breakfast room and cook your favorite meals in the well-laid-out kitchen with an adjacent formal dining room, for those large communal gatherings. The living/family room invites reading by the fire or watching your favorite TV shows. A full hall bath serves the two rooms off the primary bedroom, one of which is set up as an office and the other as a den, a room easily converted to a fourth bedroom with the addition of a set of closet doors in the nook area. So many options. The primary bedroom is a light-filled spacious retreat in the rear of the home, with views of the private back yard and direct access to the lovely blue-stone patio. The ensuite primary bath has double sinks, custom cabinetry and a tiled shower with a seating area. Two large rooms, a full bath and additional storage areas round out the living spaces in the home upstairs. The basement is shelved for all your storage needs, and houses the utilities, including an 88-gallon water heater and a double-walled, corrosion-resistant oil tank. (Homes nearby have converted to gas if that suits you.) The attached 2-car garage with pull-down stairs to a floored attic provides tons of extra space for all of your sports equipment and gardening implements. The location really cannot be beat. Medical facilities, luxury retail shops, abundant public gardens, trails, parks and two commuter rail stations are all only minutes away. This home has it all and will not last. Come see for yourself what your next stop in life could be.

A  mudroom is located just beyond the kitchen & makes a lovely transition to the back stair hall and side porch entry. With five acres of gorgeous private grounds, including a former green house, Houstonia is also surrounded by an oasis of township parkland in Wallingford. PublicRemarks: Welcome to 341 Owen Avenue, a rare and versatile opportunity tucked away in the desirable Drexel Plaza section of Upper Darby Township. Situated on a spacious double lot, this 5-bedroom, 2.5-bath home is full of character and offers plenty of room to spread out across three floors, with a layout that can easily adapt to both everyday living and those who need extra space for work and/or hobbies. As you arrive, you simply cannot miss the privacy and curb appeal. The front of the home is adorned with stately columns, lush landscaping, and a charming wooden fence that leads to a spacious front yard. As you enter, the front sunroom and inviting foyer set the tone of warmth and welcome.  The spacious living room features beautiful crown molding and is anchored by a beautiful stone fireplace—perfect for both everyday comfort and entertaining. The formal dining room flows seamlessly into a charming breakfast nook with built-in shelving, a brick facade and built in bench seating.  The modern kitchen boasts ample cabinetry, lots of counter space and a unique serving window which connects to the formal dining room, making 341 Owen Avenue an entertainer's dream. Unique to the home are two separate staircases—one off the main living area and another tucked privately near the breakfast room—adding both charm and convenience. The first level offers a large powder room and a convenient laundry area. On the second level, you will find a large primary bedroom, spacious enough for not only your bedroom furniture but also a sitting area or even a work-from-home station. The second bedroom on this level offers the bonus of additional flex space, which could work as a large closet, office, playroom, workout area...etc. The second floor is complete with a third well-appointed bedroom and one full bath. Make your way to the third floor and you will find two additional generously-sized bedrooms and another full bath. This level is perfect to house your guests, older children, in-laws, or to have a playroom, office, home gym... the choice is all yours! The basement has been fully waterproofed, offering a dry, clean space that is perfect for storage and peace of mind. Outdoors, the property truly sets itself apart. Get ready to host all of your friends and family for summer barbecues! The large double lot provides an abundance of space and privacy which is unique to the area. Complete with a large patio, a charming greenhouse, and expansive yard, you will feel like you are truly tucked away in your very own backyard oasis. And you can do so much with this space! For contractors, business owners, or hobbyists, the property offers unmatched utility with parking for up to 8–10 vehicles and three separate electric-equipped sheds—one of which is large enough to accommodate two vehicles or serve as a workshop and so much more. Whether you're seeking an expansive and beautiful residence or a property that supports your lifestyle, 341 Owen Avenue presents a unique blend of elegance, charm, and opportunity in a well-established community.  Don't miss the opportunity to call this home yours. Schedule your private tour today! ***OPEN HOUSES: Friday 4/10/26 from 4pm-6pm and Saturday 4/11/26 from 11am-1pm***

PublicRemarks: Rarely does a property such as this become available.  Words can describe the structures and setting, but to appreciate what it has to offer you must experience it in person.  This five acre property in Thornbury Township was originally part of the Cheyney Estate.  Along with the close proximity to town amenities, you have expansive amounts of breathing room to savor all of the history that surrounds you.  A portion of the main residence dates back to approximately 1740, and if those stone walls could talk, can you just imagine what you would learn?  You approach the home along a private driveway and the landscaped roundabout.  You are immediately surrounded by a plethora of beauty created by the abundance of mature perennials and flowering trees.  Throughout the spring, summer and fall they will continue to surprise and delight your senses with a continuous burst of color and new life. This beauty delivers you to the front entrance of the main residence where the first floor boasts an open concept living room, dining room, small alcove with built in cabinets, and family room that is predominantly windows inviting you to delight in the vast views.  Completing the first floor is the kitchen and breakfast room, guest bedroom, and primary suite with stained glass windows and hand hewn beams, a walk-in shower, dressing room, walk-in closet and access to the rear deck.  If you are looking for a one floor living option, you’ve found it!   Continuing to the second floor you will be greeted by an open transitional space with skylights that screams reading nook.  There is a large bedroom with ample closet space, a hall bath with an imported onyx pedestal sink, another good size bedroom, and finally the bonus room with private access to the outside. This large space with parquet flooring can be used as a fifth bedroom, an amazing home office, a studio space with good natural light, a recreation room or teenager’s retreat.  The options are endless! We now need to explore the lower level which is ground level at the rear.  Here you will find a TV/playroom with those spectacular stone walls, hand hewn beams, and a wonderful butler’s pantry space with access to the outside for al fresco parties.  On this level there is also a full bath, sauna, laundry room with storage and pantry space.  Finally on this level is access to a two car garage to house your classic cars. There is another surprise treat before we venture on - an attached, one bedroom apartment with a separate entrance in the rear.  Think in-laws, guests, nanny, etc. Continuing our journey outside, you will first encounter the stone icehouse which was built in the 18th century.  It has electricity, a small gas stove, and a waterfall (depending upon that years water table) gracing one of the inside stone walls.  Just imagine relaxing here with your favorite beverage at the end of a long day.  Better yet, the downstairs has the potential for your personal wine cellar with it’s year round temperature that remains close to 55 degrees.  If you can tear yourself away from this little gem, you’ll find the main barn.  Go inside this Civil War structure and become awestruck!  Imagine the graduation parties, baby showers or barn dances you can have here.  The possibilities are endless!  There is also a side barn which is a great area for storage and workshop use.  Adjacent to the side barn is a hardscape, kidney shaped patio that is perfect for those firepit evenings.  The patio overlooks approximately three acres of partially fenced pasture, perfect for wildlife viewing and leisure space for your animals.  Stroll to the rear of the barn and you’ll find horse stalls and two garages along with gorgeous stone ruins near the natural spring just waiting to become a walled garden or huge patio. Envision how you would live here.   The main source of heat is a heat pump while the separate apartment has electric heat.  There is propane for cooking and hot water.

The updated kitchen with stainless steel appliances and refreshed baths, including a classic claw-foot tub, balance historic charm with modern comfort. Outdoor living is enhanced by a newly constructed rear deck with hot tub and a new all-season porch addition overlooking the property. The detached oversized two-car garage was fully renovated two years ago with new siding and a new roof. Ideally positioned just off Route 202 and within approximately one mile of major shopping, dining, Whole Foods, Wegmans, Brinton Lake, and everyday conveniences, the property offers close proximity to everything while maintaining a peaceful, estate-like setting. PublicRemarks: Step into 57 Benson Drive and discover a lifestyle that makes homeownership feel effortless. Nestled in one of Delaware County’s most established and charming cooperative communities, this two-bedroom, one-bath interior townhome offers the rare combination of peace, convenience, and financial predictability. Built in 1945, this community has been meticulously maintained, offering residents a serene environment while keeping life simple, comfortable, and worry-free. Here, you don’t just buy a home, you join a cooperative that handles almost everything for you. Imagine this: your utilities are covered, your roof, boiler, and hot water heater are maintained, and exterior upkeep is handled automatically. Your only responsibility is the small joy of caring for your own fenced yard. No more sweating over electric bills in the dead of winter, no worrying about whether the water usage went too high, and no surprise maintenance costs. Every month, your $578 fee covers it all, allowing you to focus on living, not fixing. Step inside this inviting interior unit, and you immediately notice the thoughtful layout and natural flow of the space. To your right, the utility room keeps all home systems organized and provides the convenience of main floor laundry. To your left, the well-appointed kitchen and dining area are ready for cozy family meals or entertaining friends. Step further in, and the living room opens up with sunlight streaming through large windows, framing the backyard and screened-in porch, the perfect spot for summer evenings, morning coffee, or quiet afternoons immersed in a book. Outside, the fenced yard has been beautifully maintained and includes a pristinely kept shed, ready for hobbies, a workshop, or your very own outdoor man cave or she shed. Every detail has been considered to maximize comfort, convenience, and enjoyment. Upstairs, the updated full bathroom is sleek and modern, while both bedrooms provide ample space and storage. The master bedroom boasts large windows that fill the room with natural light and two generous closets. Additional storage is available in the attic, giving you space for everything you need, without compromise. This cooperative lifestyle goes beyond the walls of your home. Enjoy a peaceful, walkable neighborhood while remaining conveniently close to shopping, dining, and public transportation.  57 Benson Drive isn’t just a home. It’s an invitation to live effortlessly, confidently, and fully, to step into a space where maintenance worries disappear, monthly bills are predictable, and every season brings comfort and ease. If you’ve been dreaming of a home that offers charm, convenience, and true peace of mind, this is the one. Schedule your appointment today!!  IMPORTANT DETAILS: This property is offered for OWNER-OCCUPANT, CASH-ONLY PURCHASES. No Financing Accepted (no exceptions). PublicRemarks: Welcome to “The Edgar House”, a landmark Federal-style residence in the heart of Newtown Square. Originally constructed in 1840 and thoughtfully expanded in 1910 and 2008. Seamlessly blending early American architecture with modern livability, this extraordinary estate offers timeless character paired with exceptional comfort. Privately set on 1.3 lush acres, the home greets you with a circular driveway and charming covered porch. Spanning three stories plus a finished walk-out lower level, the residence features 6 bedrooms, 5 full baths, 3 half baths, and more than five flexible living spaces designed to evolve with today’s lifestyle needs. Historic integrity remains beautifully preserved throughout, including remarkably thick stone walls, natural wood floors, intricate crown moldings, multiple fireplaces, and dual front and rear staircases leading to the second floor. The main level showcases a formal dining room anchored by a handsome stone fireplace and a gracious living room with rich millwork and a wood-burning fireplace. The updated eat-in kitchen is a chef’s dream, featuring a Viking appliance package with double oven, 6-burner gas range with griddle and hood vent, two additional wall ovens, granite countertops, a farmhouse sink and walk-in pantry. The adjoining breakfast room boasts a cathedral ceiling with exposed beam and a cozy wood-burning stove. A separate prep area adds exceptional functionality with an additional oven-range, double sink, refrigerator, and stackable washer and dryer.  Two powder rooms complete the main level. Outdoor living is equally impressive with a 1,600-square-foot wraparound deck and patio overlooking manicured grounds framed by mature landscaping.  An ideal setting for entertaining or relaxation. The second level offers two ensuite primary bedrooms and two additional bedrooms and a shared hall bath. The third level provides two more bedrooms, a full bathroom and a flexible sitting area. Perfect for guests, office space or creative pursuits. The finished walk-out lower level delivers remarkable versatility, offering multiple living areas, a full bath, half bath, kitchenette/wet bar, workshop, and abundant storage. Ideal for remote work, virtual learning, hobbies, multi-generational living or au pair accommodations. A rare interior smoke room offers a distinctive nod to the home’s storied past, while an updated shingle roof provides peace of mind. The climate-controlled three-car detached garage is perfect for collectors or hobbyists. Located within the highly sought-after Marple Newtown School District, the property is walkable to shopping, dining, and recreation. Enjoy nearby Ellis Preserve, home to Whole Foods Market, La Scala's Fire, Teca, First Watch, Paris Baguette, Sedona Taphouse, and Ruth's Chris Steak House. Golf enthusiasts will appreciate proximity to Paxon Hollow Golf Club. Nature lovers will enjoy a variety of nearby parks. Commuting is effortless with convenient access to Route 3 (West Chester Pike), Route 252 and I-476 (Blue Route). “The Edgar House” is more than a residence,  it is a preserved piece of American history, thoughtfully reimagined for modern luxury living.  **Please note: some of the images have been enhanced and/or virtually staged.**

PublicRemarks: Welcome home to the community of Darlington Woods! Why not make 211 Nottingham Ct your new address?!  211 Nottingham Ct is a 2 story condo, not a flat like some of the other units you will find here. It is a 2 bedroom, 3 bath unit with an attached one car garage with inside access. It is located in the desirable borough of Chester Heights. Facing north east, this unit receives sunlight in the front in the morning and sunlight in the back in the afternoon. Some recent updates include some fresh paint, tile flooring, newer carpets, and newer microwave and stove. Bathrooms have mostly been updated by replacing plumbing fixtures. One of the first things to notice, once inside, is the entry foyer/entry hall with the beautifully tile floor. This foyer/hallway connects this floor efficiently together. Connecting the powder room, laundry room, living room, kitchen, and breakfast room aright at the top of the hall. The dinning room is connected to the living room & kitchen on the opposite end of the home. The living room is accented by a fireplace as centerpiece and features two large windows with plantation shutters. The living has easy access to all rooms as it should. The kitchen is flanked by the dining room on one end and the breakfast room on the other. The breakfast is open to the kitchen and is only separated by a half wall. The breakfast room features 2 large windows with plantation shutters   The dining room features a sliding glass door to the rear deck patio. The view from the back is a terraced hill with trees beyond it. You can not see a neighboring property which will leave you with sense of privacy and green views 3 out of the four seasons.  Conveniently the living room, kitchen and foyer converge at the stairs leading up to the bedrooms. At the top of the stairs there is a small open space for a somewhat large decorative plant or even a bed for a furry friend if you bring one along.  This second floor consists of 2 bedrooms and 2 full baths. The large main bedroom features a private bath with double vanity, large soaking tub and a walk-in shower. The shower features a beautiful frameless glass door. There is also a walk-in closet. A second bedroom and a full hall bath complete this floor.  The Darlington Woods community includes a clubhouse, gym, tennis courts, and pool! It is very quiet here. The location is tremendous. Darlington Woods is about 2 miles to Septa's Regional Rail line (Wawa Station). Less than five miles in either direction you will find Riddle Hospital, Wollfes Apple House (locally grown & produced products), The Glen Mills golf course, the Glen Mills School, The Promenade at Granite Run, Costco, Brinton Lake Shops, Newlin Grist Mill, the public library and schools and so much more.  An extra added bonus for nature buffs, depending on the time of year, you will see the occasional white tail deer or red fox out back. Make your appointment today!

PublicRemarks: Welcome to Okehampton, a rare and extraordinary Villanova estate built in the architectural vernacular of the Gloucestershire Cotswolds in 1931 by Herbert S. Casey, the first President of the Radnor Historical Society. Thoughtfully restored over a meticulous three-year renovation by Top Notch Custom Homes, this landmark residence has been returned to its original grandeur while seamlessly incorporating today’s most desirable modern amenities.  Set on a private 1.36-acre lot in the heart of Villanova, the home beautifully blends old-world craftsmanship with refined new finishes. Exterior features include original Pennsylvania fieldstone, a restored Ludowici tile roof with new copper valleys and flashings, Hardie plank siding and standing-seam metal roofing on additions, massive brick chimneys, copper gutters and downspouts, and painted Boral trim surrounding new Andersen Architectural Series dark bronze casement windows.  The home was fully gutted to the studs, with interior spaces reimagined and expanded to create a well-balanced floor plan ideal for both everyday living and elegant entertaining. Extensive improvements include new interior framing; wire mesh and masonry stucco on basement walls; closed-cell spray foam insulation throughout; new 320-amp underground electrical service with new wiring and subpanels; all new plumbing supply, drain, and sewer lines; four high-efficiency Bryant HVAC systems with Honeywell WiFi thermostats plus a mini-split; new natural gas and water services; underground drainage tied to all downspouts; and professional basement waterproofing with interior drainage systems and dual sump pumps.  Offering 5,938 square feet of luxurious above-grade living space, the home features five bedrooms, four full and one-half baths, a two-car garage pre-wired for an electric vehicle charger, and ample driveway parking for five additional vehicles without obstructing garage access. Outdoor highlights include a stunning stone courtyard with soaring stone privacy walls and brick accents, stone steps leading to the front yard, and impeccably manicured landscape beds with Belgian block borders.  The grounds were thoughtfully landscaped with perennial plantings, mulched beds on all sides of the home, Green Giant arborvitaes for privacy, and sodded front, side, and partial rear yards. Significant care was taken to preserve original architectural details, including restored doors, exposed wood beams, slate hallway floors and steps, brick flooring, exposed stone walls, deep windowsills, and a beautifully reconstructed staircase with white oak treads, iron spindles, and a brass handrail.  This move-in-ready masterpiece is ideally located near major commuter routes, premier private schools, Aronimink Golf Club, Overbrook Country Club, shopping, dining, and is part of the highly regarded Radnor School District.  A truly exceptional home where historic elegance meets modern perfection.  Seller is a licensed real estate broker and agent has a financial interest in the property.

PublicRemarks: In the heart of historic Chadds Ford, a Pennsylvania stone masterpiece by Dewson Construction stands gracefully on the banks of Karver Lake. Marrying timeless craftsmanship with modern livability, the newly renovated home celebrates its 7-acre waterfront setting with architecture designed by Archer & Buchanan—honoring Georgian traditions while embracing light, scale, and sweeping views of the lake. The stonework of the original residence was carefully matched and carried into the home’s facades and garden walls, grounding the estate in its historic heritage. Inside, natural light floods the soaring foyer and grand staircase. Reclaimed white oak floors, richly stained, stretch across the main and upper levels, their warm tones providing classic contrast to the soft, neutral palette. Ten-foot ceilings, paneled doors, and historically reproduced hardware elevate every room with a sense of substance and authenticity.  Just beyond the entrance, an elegant office makes working from home effortless, while a pair of original fireplaces anchors the gathering room, enhanced by coffered ceilings and dining space suited for both casual evenings and elegant entertaining. A sun-filled retreat framed by an original stone wall and expansive windows provides a comfortable space for relaxation, study, or play. At the heart of the home, the newly renovated kitchen is a study in understated luxury—rich custom cabinetry, finely integrated appliances, and a blend of stone and wood counters come together in perfect harmony. Meals are served at the bay-window table or grand marble counter, all open to the adjoining family space with cozy seating groups by the stone fireplace, or the stunning lake view. French doors open to sweeping lawns and water views, inviting seamless indoor-outdoor living. A mudroom with custom cubbies, family and formal powder rooms, walk-in pantry, scullery, and abundant storage offer practical elegance. Upstairs, the primary suite evokes the calm of a luxury hotel with spacious walk-in closets and a spa-inspired bath. Three additional en suite bedrooms, an au pair suite with private sitting room, and a second-floor laundry serve family and guests with ease. The third floor extends the home’s versatility with two bedrooms, a full bath, and flexible bonus space. The daylight lower level is dedicated to wellness and recreation, featuring a fully appointed fitness suite with steam shower and sauna, full bar, lounge, and theatre. Outside, an accessory three-bay carriage house complements the attached two-car garage, offering ample space for vehicles, hobbies, and garden equipment. Every detail of this lakefront home reflects the artistry of its craftsmen, blending old-world character with state-of-the-art systems for comfort and efficiency. The result is a one-of-a-kind residence that is as elegant as it is livable, as timeless as it is current.
